free instant test

Stop at Exactly 10.00 Seconds

Start the clock, feel ten seconds pass, stop it at exactly 10.00. The catch: the final digits hide while it runs, so you’re navigating on your internal clock alone. Seeing 9.98 on the result screen is the most “one more try” feeling we know. Perfect 10.00s do happen — rarely.

▶  START THE TEST
Counts as sharpwithin ±0.15s
Genuinely impressivewithin ±0.05s
The perfect runexactly 10.00
What it testsyour internal clock

Questions

How accurate is the human internal clock?

Over a 10-second span, most people land within a few tenths of a second once they’ve calibrated with a couple of tries. Within ±0.05s is genuinely sharp.

Any trick to stopping at exactly 10 seconds?

Count with a steady anchor — many people use “one-one-thousand” pacing or a song they know at 60 BPM — and commit to the tap instead of hesitating at the end.
